title: Add character allowlist to validate_project_id to match run_id safety guarantees
analysis: validate_project_id (validation.py:28-35) only checks for empty/whitespace, while validate_run_id (validation.py:38-59) enforces a strict [a-zA-Z0-9_-]+ allowlist via fullmatch(). Both identifiers are interpolated into reconciliation stage prompts via f-strings (base.py:112-122, memory_consolidator.py:116-141, task_knowledge_sync.py:71-152). This asymmetry means a project_id containing newlines, quotes, or backticks would pass validation and distort prompt structure. The fix is mechanical: add a _PROJECT_ID_PATTERN constant and a fullmatch() check to validate_project_id, mirroring the existing run_id approach. require_project_id (validation.py:68-71) delegates to validate_project_id via the walrus-operator pattern, so it inherits the fix automatically. All 15+ MCP tool handlers in tools.py use validate_project_id and need no changes — the function signature and return shape are unchanged. Existing callers pass values like 'dark_factory' which already match the allowlist.

steps.description: Add failing tests to TestValidateProjectId for special character rejection: newline-in-middle ('proj\nid'), backtick ('proj`id'), double-quote ('proj"id'), single-quote ("proj'id"), curly-brace ('proj{id}'), semicolon ('proj;id'), dollar-sign ('proj$id'), space-in-middle ('proj id'). Each asserts result is not None with error_type 'ValidationError'. All 8 tests will FAIL since current code accepts these inputs.

In validation.py: (1) Add _PROJECT_ID_PATTERN = re.compile(r'[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+') after line 15. (2) Update validate_project_id to add a fullmatch() check after the empty/whitespace guard, returning an error dict with message mentioning allowed characters (matching run_id error style). (3) Update docstrings for validate_project_id and require_project_id to mention character restrictions. All step-1 tests now PASS.

Add trailing-newline regression test to TestValidateProjectId: test_trailing_newline_returns_error('dark_factory\n') with assertion message explaining the fullmatch vs match distinction (mirrors TestValidateRunId.test_trailing_newline_returns_error at line 100). PASSES immediately — guards against future regression to match().

Add injection-vector test to TestRequireProjectId: test_injection_vector_newline_raises_valueerror asserts ValueError is raised for 'proj\nid' (mirrors TestRequireRunId.test_injection_vector_newline_raises_valueerror at line 198). PASSES immediately — confirms require_project_id delegates correctly and inherits the allowlist check.

Add error-message content test to TestValidateProjectId: test_character_rejection_error_mentions_allowed_chars asserts the error message contains 'invalid characters' and 'ASCII letters' (matching run_id error wording). Also add test_returns_error_dict_not_raises (mirrors run_id's version at line 92) to verify no exception is raised for malicious input like '\x00bad'. PASSES immediately.

Add positive regression tests to TestValidateProjectId confirming existing-pattern IDs still pass: test_numeric_only_returns_none ('42'), test_uppercase_returns_none ('DARK_FACTORY'), test_mixed_case_hyphen_underscore_returns_none ('My-Project_1'). PASSES immediately — guards against over-restrictive pattern changes.

design_decisions.decision: Use a separate _PROJECT_ID_PATTERN constant rather than sharing _RUN_ID_PATTERN
design_decisions.rationale: Although the regex is identical today ([a-zA-Z0-9_-]+), project_id and run_id are semantically distinct identifiers. Separate constants allow independent evolution (e.g., project_id might later allow dots for namespacing) without coupling.

design_decisions.decision: Use fullmatch() not match() for the pattern check
design_decisions.rationale: Python's re.match() with $ anchor has a known newline bypass: $ matches just before a trailing newline. The existing run_id validation already uses fullmatch() with an explicit regression test (test_validation.py:100-112). Consistent use of fullmatch() prevents this class of bypass for project_id as well.

design_decisions.decision: Keep the empty/whitespace guard as the first check before the pattern check
design_decisions.rationale: The pattern fullmatch() would reject empty strings too, but keeping the explicit empty check produces a clearer error message ('project_id is required and must be non-empty' vs 'contains invalid characters'). This matches the two-tier structure in validate_run_id.

design_decisions.decision: Do not allow dots in the pattern
design_decisions.rationale: The task specifies 'same as _RUN_ID_PATTERN or similar'. Keeping the identical [a-zA-Z0-9_-]+ pattern ensures consistency. All known project_id values ('dark_factory') match this pattern. Dots can be added later if a use case arises.

design_decisions.decision: No changes to callers (tools.py, base.py, stage files)
design_decisions.rationale: validate_project_id's signature and return shape (dict | None) are unchanged. require_project_id delegates via walrus operator and inherits the fix. All 15+ MCP tool handlers and BaseStage.run() continue to work without modification.

reuse.how: Clone the exact same regex ([a-zA-Z0-9_-]+) and fullmatch() check structure for project_id. Error dict format matches existing convention: {'error': str, 'error_type': 'ValidationError'}.

reuse.what: TestValidateRunId test patterns for special character rejection
reuse.how: Mirror the same test structure: one test per injection vector, trailing-newline regression test with explanatory assertion message, error-dict-not-raises safety test. Reuse same assertion pattern: assert result is not None + assert result['error_type'] == 'ValidationError'.

require_project_id already delegates to validate_project_id via `if err := validate_project_id(...)`. No code change needed — only docstring update. The TestRequireProjectId injection test confirms this delegation works correctly.
